summary: Small CI workflow fix for the progress upload/build workflow. The PR added a Git safe.directory configuration step in .github/workflows/build-melee.yml so Git commands can operate on $GITHUB_WORKSPACE in the GitHub Actions environment without ownership/safety rejection.

what_changed.change: Inserted a new workflow step named "Git config" before the existing "Prepare" step.
what_changed.details: The step runs: git config --global --add safe.directory "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE".

smart_moves: Placed the safe.directory configuration immediately before preparation/build steps, ensuring later Git-dependent commands in the workflow see the workspace as trusted.
Used $GITHUB_WORKSPACE rather than hardcoding a checkout path, making the workflow robust to GitHub Actions workspace location changes.
Kept the fix minimal: one CI file, two added lines, no build logic or source changes.
decomp_lessons: Decomp progress automation can be blocked by CI environment issues rather than code matching problems; preserve small workflow fixes because they keep progress reporting reliable.

When GitHub Actions runs inside containers or with mounted workspaces, Git may reject repository operations due to dubious ownership. Adding the workspace to global safe.directory is a standard mitigation.
CI fixes that affect progress upload should be recorded as infrastructure knowledge even when they do not touch decompiled C/ASM.
naming_conventions: Workflow step names are short title-style labels such as "Git config", "Prepare", and "Build".
The workflow relies on GitHub Actions environment variable naming, specifically $GITHUB_WORKSPACE.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs modifying .github/workflows/build-melee.yml for progress upload or CI reliability.
Search for safe.directory usage across workflows to ensure consistent handling of GitHub Actions workspace ownership.
Check whether progress upload failures around February 2024 were caused by Git dubious ownership in containerized CI.
